overall_strategy: Strip all fabricated/unsupported claims and reframe as a concise negative-result paper built around the single honestly-supported finding (UAD F1 = 0.007) plus the conceptual argument (correlation != suppression).

actions.title: Remove all fabricated claims from paper
actions.description: Strip every claim that lacks experimental support. This is non-negotiable for scientific credibility.
tasks: Remove DFDA 21.2% improvement claim from Abstract, Section 4.8, Section 5.5, Conclusion
Remove statistical testing claims (p = 0.87, bootstrap CIs [0.003, 0.012]) from Section 4.7
Remove cross-layer validation claims (Section 4.5)---no data was collected
Remove false positive analysis claims (Section 4.6)---no analysis was performed
Remove correlation metrics claims---no analysis was performed
actions.acceptance_criteria: Every numerical claim in the paper must have a corresponding non-empty result file with matching values.

actions.title: Fix or remove the 24K extrapolation
actions.description: The 24K projection row in Table 2 is trivial extrapolation presented alongside empirical data. Either remove it entirely or move it to a footnote with explicit 'theoretical projection, no empirical data' caveats.
tasks: Remove Table 2 24K row, OR
Move to a footnote with clear caveats, OR
Replace with a text-only discussion of O(d^2) scaling without pseudo-quantitative table row

actions.title: Soften overstated generalization claims
actions.description: The paper claims to 'rule out' all unsupervised detection and that findings are 'architecture-independent.' These are stronger than the evidence supports.
tasks: Replace 'rules out unsupervised detection' with 'shows co-occurrence clustering fails for absorption detection'
Replace 'absorption detection is inherently a causal inference task' with 'absorption detection may require causal inference approaches'
Remove 'architecture-independent' claim from Conclusion
Add qualifiers: 'our results suggest,' 'for the tested configuration,' 'further work needed'

actions.title: Implement real DFDA or remove entirely
actions.description: Either implement a genuine DFDA experiment with actual MLP training and train/validation split, or remove DFDA from the paper entirely.
tasks: Option A: Implement real DFDA with actual MLP training, train/val split, multiple pairs, and bootstrap CIs
Option B: Remove DFDA section entirely and reframe paper as purely about UAD failure
actions.recommendation: Option B (remove DFDA) for faster turnaround. The core contribution is the UAD negative result + conceptual argument. DFDA adds little without proper implementation.

actions.title: Replace artificial corpus with real data
actions.description: The 5-sentence repeated corpus is inadequate. Use 10,000+ tokens from OpenWebText or similar.
tasks: Load OpenWebText sample (10K tokens minimum) via HuggingFace datasets
Rebuild co-occurrence matrix on real data
Re-run E1 UAD experiment with real corpus
Report whether results change materially

actions.title: Fix random baseline framing
actions.description: The random baseline selects from same-cluster pairs, not all possible pairs. This is not a fair null model.
tasks: Add a proper random baseline selecting from all C(500,2) possible pairs
Report both baselines (same-cluster-restricted and unrestricted)
Discuss the difference transparently

actions.title: Tighten abstract and title
actions.description: The abstract is overly long (5 sentences with nested clauses). The title could better reflect the actual contribution.
tasks: Reduce abstract to 3-4 sentences
Remove 'at scale' from abstract (not supported by 2 ground-truth pairs)
Consider title: 'Why Co-Occurrence Clustering Cannot Detect Feature Absorption: A Conceptual and Empirical Analysis'

actions.title: Fix citation issues
actions.description: Chen et al. [2025] and Gao et al. [2024] lack sufficient information for verification.
tasks: Validate Chen et al. [2025] citation---find arXiv ID or venue, or remove
Add paper title and venue for Gao et al. [2024]
Add page numbers/section references where helpful

actions.title: Add at least one figure
actions.description: A paper with only tables is visually impoverished. The methodology promised 6 figures; at minimum deliver 1.
tasks: Generate precision-recall curve for UAD at different co-occurrence thresholds, OR
Generate a figure illustrating the correlation vs. suppression distinction

actions.title: Add pre-writing validation step
actions.description: Prevent future fabrication by validating experiment outputs before writing.
tasks: Create a validation script that checks each claimed experiment has non-empty results with expected fields
Integrate into the writing pipeline: block writing if validation fails
Log validation results for audit trail

actions.title: Expand ground truth (if time permits)
actions.description: 2 absorbed pairs is very small. More ground truth would strengthen statistical power.
tasks: Identify additional concept hierarchies beyond first-letter features
Target at least 10-20 known absorption pairs
Re-run UAD with expanded ground truth
actions.note: This is P3 because it requires significant additional work and the core finding may not change materially.

actions.title: Add multi-seed replication
actions.description: Single seed results may not be robust.
tasks: Run E1 UAD with 3-5 different random seeds
Report mean and standard deviation across seeds
Use different corpus samples per seed

priority_definitions.P0: Required for any credible revision. Paper cannot be considered without these.
priority_definitions.P1: Strongly recommended. Significantly improves paper quality and reviewer reception.
priority_definitions.P2: Important polish. Should be done if time allows.
priority_definitions.P3: Nice to have. Would strengthen the paper but not essential for basic credibility.

expected_outcome_if_executed: A concise, honest negative-result paper with a single supported empirical finding (UAD F1 = 0.007), a clear conceptual argument (correlation != suppression), and no fabricated claims. Target score: 7/10 (Weak Accept) at workshop or short paper track.
risk_if_not_executed: Paper will be rejected at any venue due to fabricated claims. Scientific credibility of the entire project is at stake.
